frozen_components: Answer set programming (ASP) logic program structure
architecture.summary: SLASH integrates neural networks with answer set programming (ASP) via Neural-Probabilistic Predicates (NPPs). NPPs wrap trained deep models (or combinations thereof) as probabilistic predicates in a logic program. The system grounds the ASP program, prunes stochastically insignificant atoms to reduce the ground program size, and performs tractable probabilistic inference over the remaining ground rules. Information flows from neural model outputs (which estimate probabilities for ground atoms) into logical inference, unified through ASP semantics.
trained_components: Neural-Probabilistic Predicates (NPPs) — trained deep models of any type

claims.statement: SLASH allows for integration of tractable probabilistic inference within a Deep Probabilistic Programming Language
NPPs are a novel design principle allowing for combining all deep model types and combinations thereof to be represented as a single probabilistic predicate
A novel +/− notation is introduced for answering various types of probabilistic queries by adjusting the atom notations of a predicate
Pruning of stochastically insignificant parts of the ground program speeds up reasoning without sacrificing predictive performance
SLASH offers the power of true joint probability estimation, improving upon prior Deep Probabilistic Programming Language approaches that allow only for limited conditional probabilistic queries
